methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $66M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Chef Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Rajat Bhageria that has raised $65.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of nine companies—one public and eight private—across the robotics and manufacturing sector, including comparable-stage references to 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two methods were applied to triangulate value. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current valuation, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to the next financing stage, yielding $131M. Peer-median valuation across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in company maturity, technology readiness, and market traction within the robotics sector.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $112M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $603M. The $489M spread between low and high estimates reflects meaningful uncertainty around where Chef Robotics lands relative to its peer cohort. Key variables affecting resolution include evidence of product-market fit, customer concentration and contract value, manufacturing scalability, and whether the company is closer to Series B readiness or earlier-stage risk mitigation. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively; peer-median reflects observed market pricing but assumes comparable execution and market conditions.
